summary: This content provides a general overview of document categories that may be requested from self-employed borrowers. It is education-only and does not collect personal financial details, determine eligibility, or provide loan origination decisions.

key_steps.title: Confirm what your lender requests
key_steps.instruction: Self-employed documentation needs vary. Use your lender’s portal or your Loan Officer’s instructions as the source of truth for what to provide.

key_steps.title: Gather business and income documentation
key_steps.instruction: Collect the business and income documents requested by your lender, and ensure they are complete and legible.

key_steps.title: Separate personal and business materials
key_steps.instruction: Organize documents clearly so it is easy to identify which items relate to personal vs business information.

key_steps.title: Submit through the approved secure channel
key_steps.instruction: Upload documents through the secure portal or method provided. Avoid sharing sensitive details through unapproved channels.

faq_items.question: Why might self-employed borrowers be asked for additional documentation?
faq_items.answer: Documentation may be needed to understand income stability and business context according to lender requirements. Your Loan Officer can explain the specific requests for your situation.

faq_items.question: What if I don’t have a document that was requested?
faq_items.answer: If you cannot provide an item, let your Loan Officer know and ask for guidance on acceptable alternatives within the lender’s process.

escalation_triggers.trigger_text: Here are my income numbers and bank balances—can you tell me if I qualify?
Based on my business income, will I be approved?
